segments.text: Why aren’t times laid up by the Almighty? Why don’t those who know him see his days?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: There are people who remove the landmarks. They violently take away flocks, and feed them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless, and they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They turn the needy out of the way. The poor of the earth all hide themselves.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Behold, as wild donkeys in the desert, they go out to their work, seeking diligently for food. The wilderness yields them bread for their children.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They cut their food in the field. They glean the vineyard of the wicked.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They lie all night naked without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for lack of a shelter.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: There are those who pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
so that they go around naked without clothing. 
Being hungry, they carry the sheaves.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They make oil within the walls of these men. They tread wine presses, and suffer thirst.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
From out of the populous city, men groan. The soul of the wounded cries out, 
yet God doesn’t regard the folly.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“These are of those who rebel against the light. They don’t know its ways, 
nor stay in its paths.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The murderer rises with the light. He kills the poor and needy. 
In the night he is like a thief.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The eye also of the adulterer waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye will see me.’
He disguises his face.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
In the dark they dig through houses. They shut themselves up in the daytime. 
They don’t know the light.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For the morning is to all of them like thick darkness, for they know the terrors of the thick darkness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“They are foam on the surface of the waters. Their portion is cursed in the earth. 
They don’t turn into the way of the vineyards.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Drought and heat consume the snow waters, so does Sheol[^11] those who have sinned.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
The womb will forget him. The worm will feed sweetly on him. 
He will be no more remembered. 
Unrighteousness will be broken as a tree.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He devours the barren who don’t bear. He shows no kindness to the widow.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yet God preserves the mighty by his power. He rises up who has no assurance of life.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: God gives them security, and they rest in it. His eyes are on their ways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
They are exalted; yet a little while, and they are gone. Yes, they are brought low, they are taken out of the way as all others, 
and are cut off as the tops of the ears of grain.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If it isn’t so now, who will prove me a liar, and make my speech worth nothing?
segments.speaker: Unknown
